is an American [rock](/wiki/Rock_music "Rock music") singer and songwriter. She began her career singing backup for [Pat Benatar](/wiki/Pat_Benatar "Pat Benatar"), then took her distinctive and powerful voice to the indie/alternative scene and joined the ever-evolving line-up of [Golden Palominos](/wiki/Golden_Palominos "Golden Palominos") from 1985 through 1987, appearing on their second and third albums. She left the group in 1987 to establish her solo career.[[1]](#cite_note-KCRW-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American rock singer and songwriter (born 1958)

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/de/Syd_Straw_at_the_Orpheum_3.jpg/250px-Syd_Straw_at_the_Orpheum_3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Syd_Straw_at_the_Orpheum_3.jpg)

Syd Straw at the Orpheum Theatre (2012)

**Syd Straw** is an American [rock](/wiki/Rock_music "Rock music") singer and songwriter. She began her career singing backup for [Pat Benatar](/wiki/Pat_Benatar "Pat Benatar"), then took her distinctive and powerful voice to the indie/alternative scene and joined the ever-evolving line-up of [Golden Palominos](/wiki/Golden_Palominos "Golden Palominos") from 1985 through 1987, appearing on their second and third albums. She left the group in 1987 to establish her solo career.[[1]](#cite_note-KCRW-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Syd_Straw&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Straw is the daughter of actor Jack Straw (*[The Pajama Game](/wiki/The_Pajama_Game "The Pajama Game")*) and songwriter Barrie Jean Garvin.

She was a frequent lead singer and occasional co-songwriter for Golden Palominos, which was spearheaded by drummer [Anton Fier](/wiki/Anton_Fier "Anton Fier") and also featured vocal turns by [Michael Stipe](/wiki/Michael_Stipe "Michael Stipe"), [Matthew Sweet](/wiki/Matthew_Sweet "Matthew Sweet"), [Don Dixon](/wiki/Don_Dixon_(musician) "Don Dixon (musician)"), [Jack Bruce](/wiki/Jack_Bruce "Jack Bruce") and others.

She appeared as a featured musical guest on many programs, including the initial episode of MTV's *Unplugged*, *The Tonight Show with Jay Leno*, and *Late Night with David Letterman*.

Straw in 1989 released her first solo album, *[Surprise](/wiki/Surprise_(Syd_Straw_album) "Surprise (Syd Straw album)")*,[[2]](#cite_note-2) which featured contributions by Stipe, Fier, [Richard Thompson](/wiki/Richard_Thompson_(musician) "Richard Thompson (musician)"), [Jody Harris](/wiki/Jody_Harris "Jody Harris"), [Peter Blegvad](/wiki/Peter_Blegvad "Peter Blegvad"), and a number of other well-known musicians. She released a second solo album, *[War and Peace](/wiki/War_and_Peace_(Syd_Straw_album) "War and Peace (Syd Straw album)")*, in 1996. Recorded with [Midwest](/wiki/American_Midwest "American Midwest") rock group The Skeletons, it chronicled a brief and unhappy romance. In 2008, she released a new album, *Pink Velour,* on her own label, Earnester Records.[[1]](#cite_note-KCRW-1) According to an earlier interview, the title track "is about my family moving back and forth between the east and the west, and my being kidnapped by my mother on Valentine's Day in 1969, and about not seeing my dad for almost seven years."

A frequent [backup singer](/wiki/Backup_singer "Backup singer") and contributor to [duets](/wiki/Duet_(music) "Duet (music)") with other musicians, Straw has also maintained an intermittent acting career, appearing on the television shows *[The Adventures of Pete & Pete](/wiki/The_Adventures_of_Pete_%26_Pete "The Adventures of Pete & Pete")* and the original *[Tales of the City](/wiki/Tales_of_the_City "Tales of the City")*. Straw's role on *The Adventures of Pete & Pete*, as middle-school algebra teacher Miss Fingerwood, was introduced in the episode "Valentine's Day Massacre," and was reprised in the episodes "Hard Day's Pete" and "x=why?"

Syd Straw can be heard singing harmony and backing vocals on the [Rickie Lee Jones](/wiki/Rickie_Lee_Jones "Rickie Lee Jones") albums *[Traffic from Paradise](/wiki/Traffic_from_Paradise "Traffic from Paradise")* and *[The Evening of My Best Day](/wiki/The_Evening_of_My_Best_Day "The Evening of My Best Day")*. She is also featured on [Leo Kottke](/wiki/Leo_Kottke "Leo Kottke")'s *[Peculiaroso](/wiki/Peculiaroso "Peculiaroso"),* which Jones produced. Straw appears on "What Am I Worth" from the [Dave Alvin](/wiki/Dave_Alvin "Dave Alvin") album *[King of California](/wiki/King_of_California "King of California")*. For a number of years she has given an annual "Heartwreck" live performance on Valentine's Day.

In 1994, Straw teamed up with [Wilco](/wiki/Wilco "Wilco") to contribute the song "The T.B. Is Whipping Me" to the [AIDS](/wiki/AIDS "AIDS") benefit album *[Red Hot + Country](/wiki/Red_Hot_%2B_Country "Red Hot + Country")*, produced by the [Red Hot Organization](/wiki/Red_Hot_Organization "Red Hot Organization"). She continues to record and perform today, recently in San Francisco's legendary [Hardly Strictly Bluegrass](https://www.hardlystrictlybluegrass.com/) festival in September 2023.

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Syd_Straw&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Discography")]

### Albums

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Syd_Straw&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Albums")]

* 1989: *[Surprise](/wiki/Surprise_(Syd_Straw_album) "Surprise (Syd Straw album)")* ([Virgin](/wiki/Virgin_Records "Virgin Records")) – reissued in 2000 on Koch with two bonus tracks
* 1996: *[War and Peace](/wiki/War_and_Peace_(Syd_Straw_album) "War and Peace (Syd Straw album)")* (Capricorn)
* 2001: *Live at the Triple Crown* (TAO) with The Adventures Of...
* 2005: *Whole Wide World: Uncollected Songs* (self-released)
* 2008: *Pink Velour* (Earnester)

### Compilation albums

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Syd_Straw&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Compilation albums")]

* 1985: various artists – *Luxury Condos Coming to Your Neighborhood Soon* (Coyote) – track 13, "Listening To Elvis"
* 1988: various artists – *[Stay Awake: Various Interpretations of Music from Vintage Disney Films](/wiki/Stay_Awake:_Various_Interpretations_of_Music_from_Vintage_Disney_Films "Stay Awake: Various Interpretations of Music from Vintage Disney Films")* ([A&M](/wiki/A%26M_Records "A&M Records")) – track 4(c), "Blue Shadows on the Trail"
* 1990: various artists – *Rutles Highway Revisited (A Tribute to [The Rutles](/wiki/The_Rutles "The Rutles"))* ([Shimmy Disc](/wiki/Shimmy_Disc "Shimmy Disc")) – track 1–7, "I Must Be In Love" (with [Marc Ribot](/wiki/Marc_Ribot "Marc Ribot"))
* 1992: various artists – *Downtown Does The Beatles Live at the Knitting Factory 1992* (Knitting Factory) – track 10, "[Don't Let Me Down](/wiki/Don%27t_Let_Me_Down_(Beatles_song) "Don't Let Me Down (Beatles song)")" (with [Arto Lindsay](/wiki/Arto_Lindsay "Arto Lindsay"), Mark Ribot, [Joey Baron](/wiki/Joey_Baron "Joey Baron"), and Greg Cohn)
* 1993: [Chris Stamey](/wiki/Chris_Stamey "Chris Stamey") and Friends – *Christmas Time* (East Side Digital) – track 10, "(I'm Always Touched by Your) Presents, Dear"
* 1994: various artists – *Beat the Retreat – Songs by [Richard Thompson](/wiki/Richard_Thompson_(musician) "Richard Thompson (musician)")* ([Capitol](/wiki/Capitol_Records "Capitol Records")) – track 5, "For Shame of Doing Wrong" (with [Evan Dando](/wiki/Evan_Dando "Evan Dando"))
* 1994: various artists – *[Red Hot + Country](/wiki/Red_Hot_%2B_Country "Red Hot + Country")* ([Mercury Nashville](/wiki/Mercury_Nashville "Mercury Nashville")) – track 16, "The T.B. Is Whipping Me" (with [Wilco](/wiki/Wilco "Wilco"))
* 1994: various artists – *A Live Christmas Extravaganza* (Deko) – track 2, "X-Mas Twist"
* 1995: various artists – *Live from Mountain Stage Volume 8* (Blue Plate) – track 9, "Golden Dreams"
* 1998: various artists – *I WANNA BE KATE: The Songs of Kate Bush* (Brown Star Records) "The Man With The Child In His Eyes"
* 1998: various artists – *[Real: The Tom T. Hall Project](/wiki/Real:_The_Tom_T._Hall_Project "Real: The Tom T. Hall Project")* ([Sire Records](/wiki/Sire_Records "Sire Records")) – track 10: "[Harper Valley PTA](/wiki/Harper_Valley_PTA "Harper Valley PTA")" (with the Skeletons)
* 2000: various artists – *One Step Up / Two Steps Back: The Songs of [Bruce Springsteen](/wiki/Bruce_Springsteen "Bruce Springsteen")* (Capitol) – track 12, "Meeting Across the River"
* 2001: various artists – *Daddy-O Daddy! Rare Family Songs of Woody Guthrie* (Rounder) – track 8, "My Daddy (Flies a Ship in the Sky)"
* 2020: various artists – *I WANNA BE KATE: The Songs of Kate Bush – Remastered and Expanded* (Brown Star Records) "The Man With The Child In His Eyes"

### Appears on

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Syd_Straw&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Appears on")]

* 1985: [The Golden Palominos](/wiki/The_Golden_Palominos "The Golden Palominos") – *[Visions of Excess](/wiki/Visions_of_Excess "Visions of Excess")* ([Celluloid](/wiki/Celluloid_Records "Celluloid Records"))
* 1986: [Beat Rodeo](/wiki/Beat_Rodeo "Beat Rodeo") – *Home in the Heart of the Beat* ([I.R.S.](/wiki/I.R.S._Records "I.R.S. Records"))
* 1986: The Golden Palominos – *[Blast of Silence (Axed My Baby for a Nickel)](/wiki/Blast_of_Silence_(Axed_My_Baby_for_a_Nickel) "Blast of Silence (Axed My Baby for a Nickel)")* (Celluloid)
* 1987: [The dB's](/wiki/The_dB%27s "The dB's") – *The Sound of Music* (I.R.S.)
* 1987: [Victoria Williams](/wiki/Victoria_Williams "Victoria Williams") – *Happy Come Home* (Geffen)
* 1988: [Eric Ambel](/wiki/Eric_Ambel "Eric Ambel") – *[Roscoe's Gang](/wiki/Roscoe%27s_Gang "Roscoe's Gang")* ([Enigma](/wiki/Enigma_Records "Enigma Records"))
* 1988: [Face to Face](/wiki/Face_to_Face_(new_wave_band) "Face to Face (new wave band)") – *One Big Day* ([Mercury](/wiki/Mercury_Records "Mercury Records"))
* 1989: [Boo Hewerdine](/wiki/Boo_Hewerdine "Boo Hewerdine") and [Darden Smith](/wiki/Darden_Smith "Darden Smith") – *Evidence* ([Chrysalis](/wiki/Chrysalis_Records "Chrysalis Records"))
* 1989: [Mark Bingham](/wiki/Mark_Bingham_(musician) "Mark Bingham (musician)") – *I Passed for Human* (Dog Gone)
* 1989: [Marshall Crenshaw](/wiki/Marshall_Crenshaw "Marshall Crenshaw") – *[Good Evening](/wiki/Good_Evening_(album) "Good Evening (album)")* (Warner Bros.)
* 1989: [Van Dyke Parks](/wiki/Van_Dyke_Parks "Van Dyke Parks") – *[Tokyo Rose](/wiki/Tokyo_Rose_(album) "Tokyo Rose (album)")* (Warner Bros.)
* 1990: [Peter Blegvad](/wiki/Peter_Blegvad "Peter Blegvad") – *King Strut and Other Stories* ([Silvertone](/wiki/Silvertone_Records_(1980) "Silvertone Records (1980)"))
* 1990: Bloomsday – *Fortuny* ([Island](/wiki/Island_Records "Island Records"))
* 1990: [Was (Not Was)](/wiki/Was_(Not_Was) "Was (Not Was)") – *[Are You Okay?](/wiki/Are_You_Okay%3F "Are You Okay?")* ([Fontana](/wiki/Fontana_Records "Fontana Records"))
* 1990: [Roy Nathanson](/wiki/Roy_Nathanson "Roy Nathanson") – *Little Fred* ([Les Disques du Crépuscule](/wiki/Les_Disques_du_Cr%C3%A9puscule "Les Disques du Crépuscule"))
* 1991: [Barkmarket](/wiki/Barkmarket "Barkmarket") – *[Vegas Throat](/wiki/Vegas_Throat "Vegas Throat")* ([Def American Recordings](/wiki/Def_American_Recordings "Def American Recordings"))
* 1991: [David Sanborn](/wiki/David_Sanborn "David Sanborn") – *Another Hand* ([Elektra](/wiki/Elektra_Records "Elektra Records"))
* 1992: [Freedy Johnston](/wiki/Freedy_Johnston "Freedy Johnston") – *[Can You Fly](/wiki/Can_You_Fly "Can You Fly")* ([Bar/None](/wiki/Bar/None_Records "Bar/None Records"))
* 1992: [Marc Ribot](/wiki/Marc_Ribot "Marc Ribot") – *[Requiem for What's His Name](/wiki/Requiem_for_What%27s_His_Name "Requiem for What's His Name")* ([Les Disques Du Crépuscule](/wiki/Les_Disques_Du_Cr%C3%A9puscule "Les Disques Du Crépuscule"))
* 1992: [Loudon Wainwright III](/wiki/Loudon_Wainwright_III "Loudon Wainwright III") - *[History](/wiki/History_(Loudon_Wainwright_III_album) "History (Loudon Wainwright III album)")* ([Charisma Records](/wiki/Charisma_Records "Charisma Records"))
* 1993: [Dave Alvin](/wiki/Dave_Alvin "Dave Alvin") – *[Museum of Heart](/wiki/Museum_of_Heart "Museum of Heart")* ([HighTone](/wiki/Hightone_Records "Hightone Records"))
* 1993: [Rickie Lee Jones](/wiki/Rickie_Lee_Jones "Rickie Lee Jones") – *[Traffic from Paradise](/wiki/Traffic_from_Paradise "Traffic from Paradise")* ([Geffen Records](/wiki/Geffen_Records "Geffen Records"))
* 1993: [Vic Chesnutt](/wiki/Vic_Chesnutt "Vic Chesnutt") – *Drunk* ([Texas Hotel](/wiki/Texas_Hotel_Records "Texas Hotel Records"))
* 1994: Dave Alvin – *[King of California](/wiki/King_of_California_(album) "King of California (album)")* (HighTone)
* 1994: Doug Legacy with the Zydeco Party Band – *King Cake Party* (Wildcat)
* 1994: [Grant McLennan](/wiki/Grant_McLennan "Grant McLennan") – *[Horsebreaker Star](/wiki/Horsebreaker_Star "Horsebreaker Star")* (Atlantic / Beggars Banquet)
* 1994: [Leo Kottke](/wiki/Leo_Kottke "Leo Kottke") – *[Peculiaroso](/wiki/Peculiaroso "Peculiaroso")* ([Private Music](/wiki/Private_Music "Private Music"))
* 1995: [Tony Trischka](/wiki/Tony_Trischka "Tony Trischka") – *World Turning* ([Rounder](/wiki/Rounder_Records "Rounder Records"))
* 1998: [Richard Buckner](/wiki/Richard_Buckner_(musician) "Richard Buckner (musician)") – *Since* ([MCA](/wiki/MCA_Records "MCA Records"))
* 2000: [John Greaves](/wiki/John_Greaves_(musician) "John Greaves (musician)") – *The Caretaker* ([Blueprint](/wiki/Voiceprint_Records "Voiceprint Records"))
* 2000: The Schramms – *100 Questions* (Blue Rose)
* 2000: [Christy McWilson](/wiki/Christy_McWilson "Christy McWilson") – *The Lucky One* (HighTone)
* 2002: [Wayne Kramer](/wiki/Wayne_Kramer_(guitarist) "Wayne Kramer (guitarist)") – *Adult World* ([MuscleTone](/wiki/Wayne_Kramer_(guitarist)#Post_MC5 "Wayne Kramer (guitarist)"))
* 2003: Rickie Lee Jones – *[The Evening of My Best Day](/wiki/The_Evening_of_My_Best_Day "The Evening of My Best Day")* ([V2](/wiki/V2_Records "V2 Records"))
* 2004: [One Ring Zero](/wiki/One_Ring_Zero "One Ring Zero") – *As Smart as We Are* (Soft Slull Press/Urban Geek Records)
* 2014: [Wilco](/wiki/Wilco "Wilco") – *[Alpha Mike Foxtrot: Rare Tracks 1994-2014](/wiki/Alpha_Mike_Foxtrot:_Rare_Tracks_1994-2014 "Alpha Mike Foxtrot: Rare Tracks 1994-2014")* ([Nonesuch](/wiki/Nonesuch_Records "Nonesuch Records"))
* 2015: [Bill Wells](/wiki/Bill_Wells_(musician) "Bill Wells (musician)") & Friends – *Nursery Rhymes* (Karaoke Kalk)
* 2021: [Michael Hearst](/wiki/Michael_Hearst "Michael Hearst") – *Songs For Unconventional Vehicles* (Urban Geek Records)
